name: Death by Chocolate with Mocha Mousse
recipeIngredient: 1 recipe Brownies, uncooked – see SIMPLY THE BEST CHOCOLATE BROWIES or use your favorite uncooked brownie recipe
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 225 °F.
Trace a 9‑inch circle on parchment paper, flip it so the pencil line faces down, and place on the baking sheet.
Prepare the cocoa meringue: Beat egg whites with cream of tartar until soft peaks form. Gradually add one cup sugar while continuing to beat; then fold in the remaining dry ingredients (cocoa, cornstarch). Fill a pastry bag and pipe a spiral into the circle.
Place in the preheated oven and bake 15 minutes. Lower temperature to 200 °F and continue baking for 2 hours and 45 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool for 45 minutes before handling.
Raise oven temperature to 325 °F while the meringue bakes.
Prepare chocolate mousse: Melt chocolate in a double boiler, keeping it at room temperature until needed. Beat cream until soft peaks form; beat egg whites until stiff, then fold in sugar until stiff peaks form. Combine one quarter of the cream with melted chocolate and whisk; add to egg whites. Fold in remaining cream and chill.
Prepare brownie layer: Grease a 9 × 1½‑inch round tin with butter and flour; spread brownie mix inside, then bake until a toothpick comes clean (about 30 minutes). Let rest five minutes, turn out, and refrigerate 15–20 minutes. Cut horizontally in half.
Prepare ganache: Heat cream to boiling, pour over chocolate, let stand 5 minutes, stirring until smooth; keep at room temperature.
Prepare mocha mousse: Melt chocolates, water, coffee, and cocoa in a double boiler (room temperature). Whip egg whites into soft peaks, add sugar, then fold in whipped cream. Fold a quarter of the egg whites into chocolate mix, then remaining into cream, then egg whites; chill.
Assemble: Place cake on baking sheet with top half brownie side up, pour ganache over it. Trim meringue to fit snugly and place top side up, pressing gently to remove air pockets. Spoon mocha mousse onto the meringue, then add remaining brownie half bottom side down; chill 30 minutes or refrigerate 1 hour.
Decorate: Pipe a star pattern around the outer edge of the cake with chocolate mousse and fill in stars on top. Chill for another 10–15 minutes.
Serve sauce warm over plates topped with mocha rum sauce, chilling at least 4 hours before serving (12 hours is ideal).
